target.title: feat(tart): desktop/VNC support via guest Screen Sharing
target.body:
## Summary

Desktop support for the tart provider (#209 shipped headless). `crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop` brings up a visible macOS session, and `crabbox webvnc --provider tart` opens it in the **browser** — no noVNC/`websockify` tooling on the guest. A native VNC client over SSH is also supported.

## How it works

- **`--desktop` acquire:** advertise `FeatureDesktop`; enable the guest's built-in macOS **Screen Sharing** (`com.apple.screensharing`) via `tart exec`. The VM still runs `--no-graphics`. No VNC password is written to the guest — Screen Sharing uses the **account's own credentials** (Apple/ARD auth).
- **`crabbox webvnc` — host-side browser bridge:** SSH-tunnel to the guest's `127.0.0.1:5900`, serve the **embedded** noVNC viewer (vendored `rfb.js` 1.7.0 + a small `vnc.html`) plus a `/websockify` WebSocket relayed to the tunnel from a local HTTP server, and open the browser. noVNC does Apple/ARD auth with the account credentials, handed to the **local viewer only** (a `127.0.0.1` URL) — never written to the guest or placed on a command line. A viewer-only `--tart-password` / `CRABBOX_TART_PASSWORD` (default `admin`) supplies them.
- **Native VNC alternative:** `ssh -L 5900:127.0.0.1:5900 admin@<lease-ip>` then `open vnc://127.0.0.1:5900`.
- **Remote control plane (controller → Mac → guest):** run `crabbox webvnc` on the Mac and forward the printed web port — `ssh -L <port>:127.0.0.1:<port> <user>@<mac>` — then open the URL locally (crabbox prints this hint).
- The `webvnc status`/`reset`/`daemon` subcommands (the Linux noVNC-daemon model) are guarded for macOS and point to `crabbox webvnc`.

**Exposure boundary:** macOS Screen Sharing binds all guest interfaces, so VNC is reachable at the guest's address on the **host-local tart network**, gated by account auth; the SSH tunnels keep the viewer on `127.0.0.1`. Documented.

## Live proof — Apple Silicon M4 Pro, tart 2.32.1, macOS 26.4

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop --slug vncbr   -> state=ready
$ crabbox webvnc --provider tart --id cbx_...
bridge: serving noVNC locally; SSH tunnel -> guest 127.0.0.1:5900
webvnc: http://127.0.0.1:5902/vnc.html?password=admin&username=admin

$ curl http://127.0.0.1:5902/vnc.html        -> crabbox WebVNC + rfb.js + /websockify
ws /websockify handshake: HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
first relayed bytes: RFB 003.889             # guest Screen Sharing relayed browser->bridge->tunnel->guest

# guarded subcommands:
$ crabbox webvnc status --provider tart ...   -> points to `crabbox webvnc` / native client
$ crabbox list/stop --provider tart ...       -> lease ready; released; clean teardown (0 VMs)
```

A real noVNC browser then completes ARD auth and renders the desktop.

## Files

`internal/providers/tart/{provider,backend,flags}.go`, `internal/cli/webvnc.go` (routing + guard), `internal/cli/macos_webvnc.go` (bridge), `internal/cli/webvnc_assets.go` + `internal/cli/webvncassets/*` (embedded viewer), `internal/cli/config.go` (viewer password), tests, `docs/providers/tart.md`.

## Not included

- Resolution/scaling controls, multi-viewer slots, a daemonized background bridge.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
